# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.

Write secure code.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Test External Contributions

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  security-check:
    name: Security Check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      approved: ${{ steps.check.outputs.approved }}
    steps:
      - name: Check if PR is from trusted source
        id: check
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}" == "${{ github.repository }}" ]]; then
            echo "approved=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          elif [[ "${{ contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'safe-to-test') }}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "approved=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "approved=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Comment on unapproved PR
        if: steps.check.outputs.approved == 'false'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            const comment = `⚠️ This PR requires manual approval before automated tests can run.
            
            A maintainer needs to review the changes and add the \`safe-to-test\` label to trigger the test workflow.
            
            **Security Notice**: We carefully review all external contributions before running automated tests to protect our CI/CD infrastructure.`;
            
            const { data: comments } = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
            });
            
            const botComment = comments.find(comment => 
              comment.user.type === 'Bot' && 
              comment.body.includes('requires manual approval')
            );
            
            if (!botComment) {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: context.issue.number,
                body: comment
              });
            }

  build-and-test:
    name: Build and Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: security-check
    if: needs.security-check.outputs.approved == 'true'
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout base repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Checkout PR code in isolated directory
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          path: pr-code
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
          cache-dependency-path: pr-code/package-lock.json

      - name: Validate package.json
        run: |
          cd pr-code
          if ! jq empty package.json 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "Invalid package.json format"
            exit 1
          fi
          
          if jq -e '.scripts.preinstall' package.json > /dev/null; then
            echo "Suspicious preinstall script detected"
            exit 1
          fi
          
          if jq -e '.scripts.postinstall' package.json > /dev/null; then
            echo "Warning: postinstall script detected, review required"
          fi

      - name: Install dependencies with audit
        run: |
          cd pr-code
          npm ci --ignore-scripts
          npm audit --audit-level=high

      - name: Run linter
        id: lint
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          cd pr-code
          npm run lint 2>&1 | tee ../lint-output.txt
          echo "exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Run tests with timeout
        id: test
        timeout-minutes: 10
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          cd pr-code
          npm test -- --coverage --maxWorkers=2 2>&1 | tee ../test-output.txt
          echo "exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Run security scan
        id: security
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          cd pr-code
          npx --yes snyk test --severity-threshold=high 2>&1 | tee ../security-output.txt || true
          echo "exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Generate test summary
        id: summary
        run: |
          LINT_STATUS="${{ steps.lint.outputs.exit_code }}"
          TEST_STATUS="${{ steps.test.outputs.exit_code }}"
          SECURITY_STATUS="${{ steps.security.outputs.exit_code }}"
          
          if [[ "$LINT_STATUS" == "0" ]] && [[ "$TEST_STATUS" == "0" ]]; then
            echo "overall=success" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "overall=failure" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi
          
          echo "lint_status=$LINT_STATUS" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "test_status=$TEST_STATUS" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "security_status=$SECURITY_STATUS" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Upload test results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: test-results
          path: |
            lint-output.txt
            test-output.txt
            security-output.txt
            pr-code/coverage/
          retention-days: 7

      - name: Post test results as comment
        if: always()
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');
            const lintStatus = '${{ steps.summary.outputs.lint_status }}';
            const testStatus = '${{ steps.summary.outputs.test_status }}';
            const securityStatus = '${{ steps.summary.outputs.security_status }}';
            const overallStatus = '${{ steps.summary.outputs.overall }}';
            
            const statusEmoji = (status) => status === '0' ? '✅' : '❌';
            const statusText = (status) => status === '0' ? 'Passed' : 'Failed';
            
            let lintOutput = '';
            let testOutput = '';
            let securityOutput = '';
            
            try {
              lintOutput = fs.readFileSync('lint-output.txt', 'utf8').slice(-2000);
            } catch (e) {
              lintOutput = 'No output available';
            }
            
            try {
              testOutput = fs.readFileSync('test-output.txt', 'utf8').slice(-2000);
            } catch (e) {
              testOutput = 'No output available';
            }
            
            try {
              securityOutput = fs.readFileSync('security-output.txt', 'utf8').slice(-2000);
            } catch (e) {
              securityOutput = 'No output available';
            }
            
            const comment = `## 🤖 Automated Test Results
            
            **Overall Status**: ${overallStatus === 'success' ? '✅ Passed' : '❌ Failed'}
            
            | Check | Status | Result |
            |-------|--------|--------|
            | Linting | ${statusEmoji(lintStatus)} | ${statusText(lintStatus)} |
            | Tests | ${statusEmoji(testStatus)} | ${statusText(testStatus)} |
            | Security Scan | ${statusEmoji(securityStatus)} | ${statusText(securityStatus)} |
            
            <details>
            <summary>📋 Lint Output</summary>
            
            \`\`\`
            ${lintOutput}
            \`\`\`
            </details>
            
            <details>
            <summary>🧪 Test Output</summary>
            
            \`\`\`
            ${testOutput}
            \`\`\`
            </details>
            
            <details>
            <summary>🔒 Security Scan Output</summary>
            
            \`\`\`
            ${securityOutput}
            \`\`\`
            </details>
            
            ---
            *Commit: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}*
            *Workflow run: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}*`;
            
            const { data: comments } = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
            });
            
            const botComment = comments.find(comment => 
              comment.user.type === 'Bot' && 
              comment.body.includes('Automated Test Results')
            );
            
            if (botComment) {
              await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                comment_id: botComment.id,
                body: comment
              });
            } else {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: context.issue.number,
                body: comment
              });
            }

      - name: Fail workflow if tests failed
        if: steps.summary.outputs.overall == 'failure'
        run: |
          echo "Tests failed. Please review the output above."
          exit 1
